causal_chain: SEU burst at T-62s corrupted GYRO_A memory registers
ADCS attitude error grew to 7.3° exceeding 0.01° threshold
FDIR triggered safe-mode entry via ADCS_ERROR flag
Multi-subsystem cascade is a low-probability alternative
V_bat and SoC_pct are within nominal limits, ruling out EPS contribution
Software fault is possible but CPU_load and Watchdog_counter are nominal
No evidence of memory corruption or thread deadlock in event log

recovery_plan.rationale: Confirm radiation-induced SEU as the initiating event before taking corrective action
recovery_plan.verify: SEU_counter value recorded and stable (no further increments)

recovery_plan.rationale: Reset gyroscope A driver to clear corrupted register state from SEU
recovery_plan.verify: Gyro_rate_degs returns valid numeric reading within [0.0, 7.0] deg/s

recovery_plan.rationale: Switch to backup gyroscope B as redundant path if GYRO_A reset fails
recovery_plan.verify: GYRO_B health status nominal and attitude error decreasing

recovery_plan.rationale: Re-establish sun-pointing attitude to restore power-positive state
recovery_plan.verify: Attitude_error_deg < 0.5° and solar array current I_sa > 5.0A

sentinel_output.reasoning_summary: SEU burst at T-62s corrupted GYRO_A registers causing attitude divergence to 7.3°. All other subsystems (EPS, OBC, TCS, COMMS) nominal. Single-event radiation fault with high confidence. Recovery: verify SEU count → reset GYRO_A → failover to GYRO_B → sun-point.
